Tests the Math.acos function to confirm it returns NaN for inputs slightly greater than 1.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.acos` function in JavaScript. It checks that the function returns `NaN` (Not-a-Number) when given an input slightly greater than 1, specifically `1.000000000000001`. The use of `assert` ensures that the test will fail if `Math.acos` does not return `NaN` for this input, which is expected behavior according to the mathematical definition of the arccosine function. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on testing this specific edge case of the `Math.acos` function.
